_meta.description: Boris / SignalDeck World Economist — Forecast Accuracy Tracker. Each weekly run reviews prior forecasts, scores outcomes, and updates calibration metrics to improve future confidence levels.
_meta.review_instructions: At the START of each weekly run: (1) Load this file. (2) For each entry in active_forecasts where outcome_date <= today, web-search for the actual outcome. (3) Score each: HIT (forecast direction + magnitude correct), PARTIAL (direction correct, magnitude off by >20%), MISS (direction wrong). (4) Move scored forecasts to history[]. (5) Recompute calibration_metrics. (6) Save updated file. (7) Reference bias_notes when setting confidence levels this week — if overconfident, reduce HIGH→MEDIUM; if underconfident, allow MEDIUM→HIGH.

calibration_metrics.bias_notes: Inaugural run — no calibration data yet. Default to stated confidence levels. Review after April 20 scoring.
calibration_metrics.calibration_target: HIGH confidence should hit ~70-80% of the time. MEDIUM should hit ~50-65%. LOW should hit ~25-40%. If HIGH is hitting >85%, confidence is being set too conservatively — elevate more forecasts to HIGH. If HIGH is hitting <60%, reduce HIGH forecasts.

active_forecasts.boris_forecast: Deadline extended 72hrs. No strike, no ceasefire. Oman back-channel diplomatic pressure continues.
active_forecasts.consensus: Deadline passes; ambiguous outcome. Market pricing ~45% partial resolution.
active_forecasts.falsification_signal: Military strike OR confirmed ceasefire = MISS on direction

active_forecasts.event: US Initial Jobless Claims (week of Apr 9)
active_forecasts.boris_forecast: 218K — slight uptick from manufacturing sector tariff disruption
Two-sided risk framing; rejects cut timeline; no policy guidance. Markets read as hawkish hold.
active_forecasts.key_level: No cut signal; no explicit Sept path
active_forecasts.falsification_signal: Any explicit dovish pivot signal or mention of upcoming cut timing = MISS

active_forecasts.boris_forecast: +0.1% MoM — post-pull-forward hangover; energy squeeze begins

−15.0 — tariff-driven order paralysis deeper than consensus
3.4% YoY — weak GBP + energy import shock; BOE cut pushed to Q3 2026 earliest
HOLD at 2.25%. Lagarde signals next cut Sept at earliest pending energy shock assessment.

active_forecasts.event: US Initial Jobless Claims (week of Apr 17)
active_forecasts.boris_forecast: 224K — continued tariff-driven manufacturing layoffs; trend established
−12.0 — consistent with Empire State signal; tariff disruption broadening
Partial shipping lane reopening announced — military escort protocol. Oil moves to $103-108. No full resolution.

learning_log.note: Inaugural forecast set. 14 forecasts across 5 categories: 3 HIGH confidence (central bank: Powell testimony, UMich sentiment, ECB decision), 10 MEDIUM, 1 LOW (geopolitical partial resolution). Calibration baseline to be established after April 20 scoring cycle. Key watch: Boris is forecasting above-consensus on all inflation metrics (CPI, EZone CPI, UK CPI) — this directional bias should be explicitly tested against actuals to detect systematic overestimation of energy pass-through speed.

systematic_biases_to_watch.inflation_overshoot_tendency: Boris currently forecasting above consensus on all 3 CPI metrics. Track whether energy pass-through is being overestimated.
systematic_biases_to_watch.growth_pessimism: Boris forecasting below-consensus on China GDP, Empire State, Philly Fed, Retail Sales. Track whether tariff drag speed is being overestimated.
systematic_biases_to_watch.central_bank_inertia: Boris forecasting 'hold' for both ECB and Fed — this is historically the safest bet. Track whether this creates overconfidence in 'no action' forecasts.
systematic_biases_to_watch.geopolitical_discount: LOW confidence assigned to Hormuz resolution — appropriate given binary nature. Geopolitical events should generally be capped at MEDIUM confidence.
